opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I read that my blood test means I have a fatal liver disease. I feel dizzy, my mind is racing, and my liver feels like it's swelling up right now.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I researched it tonight and I'm panicking that I'm going into a liver coma.

symptom_narrative.onset: about an hour ago after online research
symptom_narrative.character: palpitations, fast breathing, a racing mind, a sense things aren't real, RUQ tightness
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: derealisation, palpitations, hyperventilation, RUQ tightness; no yellow eyes, no dark urine/pale stools, no vomiting, no confusion

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: early a slow autoimmune liver condition (positive antimitochondrial antibody, normal ultrasound)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: researching liver failure online tonight; spouse present

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants to be told her liver isn't failing

communication_style.typical_message_style: rapid, fearful, cites what she read

objective_data_offered.value: the whites of my eyes look normal (not yellow) in the mirror
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self/video inspection; not a formal assessment of liver function
